link|Q55532282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saguri Shrine |画像 = [[File:雙栗神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Atemoto Ippin Hachimangū (former name) |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Saguri Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Saguri Shrine''' (さぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The seven enshrined deities are as follows. * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) *: In the {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} she appears as "Amaterasu Ōkami," and in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as "Amaterasu Ōmikami." She is a deity in {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|1=Japanese mythology}}, famous for the hiding of the heavenly rock cave ({{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}). * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) *: In the Nihon Shoki, written as "Susanoo no Mikoto" and "Susa-no-o no Mikoto"; in the Kojiki, "Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto." This is also a deity in Japanese mythology. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} (ことしろぬし) *: A deity in Japanese mythology, regarded as a child of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (おうじんてんのう) *: Called "Homuta Tennō" in the Nihon Shoki and "Homudawake-no-Mikoto" in the Kojiki. He is considered an emperor likely to have existed from the late {{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099|1=4th century}} to the early {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095|1=5th century}}. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} (ひめがみ) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (にんとくてんのう) * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) === History === Saguri Shrine is located in Sayama Saguri and is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; it is considered to be the "Saguri deity" mentioned in the entry for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 (859) in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. From the medieval period to the Meiji era, because it enshrined a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}, it was called Atemoto (Ippin) Hachimangū. It appears under that name in the late-Edo gazetteer {{ill|lt=Miyako Meisho Zue|Q11643495|WD=Q11643495|1=Miyako meisho zue}}, where it is described as "about twenty chō west of Ōkubo, within Sayama village"{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.kumiyama.lg.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=333&frmCd=3-3-1-0-0 |website=Kumiyama Town|title=Saguri Shrine|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Precincts === === Honden === The scale and style of the honden are three-bay nagare-zukuri (a honden with three front bays), reflecting enshrinement of a divided spirit from Iwashimizu Hachimangū. According to shrine tradition, the honden was likely built around 1494 ({{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 3), at the end of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress-bark roofing), and has a {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} at the front. Between the bracket complexes of the honden are carved {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} struts: "flowers and birds" on the left and "autumn leaves and deer" on the right. Above the side panels are intricate carvings featuring "squirrels and grapes." In 1980-1981 (Shōwa 55-56), repainting and reroofing were carried out. === Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === North of the honden stands a giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}, about 30 m tall with a trunk circumference of 535 cm, estimated at 400-500 years old. A {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} is wrapped around it, and a small Inari shrine at its base shows that local residents have long revered it as a sacred tree. Because this lowland area was frequently flooded, large trees are relatively rare; together with its vigorous growth, this makes it highly valuable. In June 1993 (Heisei 3), it was selected for "Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites (plant category)," and in February 1994 (Heisei 8) it was designated a town natural monument{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/flora44.html |title=Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites: camphor tree of Saguri Shrine |website=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Saguri Shrine honden === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (national registration) === * Saguri Shrine haiden, honden gate, tamagaki fence, and stone torii (structures) - nationally registered in April 2014 (Heisei 26) === Kumiyama Town designated natural monument === * Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 ;Access * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} / Keihan Uji Kōtsū Bus, get off at "Kumiyama Danchi-guchi," then about 2 minutes on foot north === Footnotes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saguri Tenjin Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} - A shrine in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ujitawara|Q1203224|WD=Q1203224|1=Ujitawara}}. Like Saguri Shrine, it is a shikinaisha. {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:久御山町の建築物]] [[Category:久御山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:京都府の重要文化財]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Atemoto Ippin Hachimangū (former name) |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Saguri Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Saguri Shrine''' (さぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The seven enshrined deities are as follows. * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) *: In the {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} she appears as "Amaterasu Ōkami," and in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as "Amaterasu Ōmikami." She is a deity in {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|1=Japanese mythology}}, famous for the hiding of the heavenly rock cave ({{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}). * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) *: In the Nihon Shoki, written as "Susanoo no Mikoto" and "Susa-no-o no Mikoto"; in the Kojiki, "Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto." This is also a deity in Japanese mythology. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} (ことしろぬし) *: A deity in Japanese mythology, regarded as a child of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (おうじんてんのう) *: Called "Homuta Tennō" in the Nihon Shoki and "Homudawake-no-Mikoto" in the Kojiki. He is considered an emperor likely to have existed from the late {{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099|1=4th century}} to the early {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095|1=5th century}}. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} (ひめがみ) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (にんとくてんのう) * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) === History === Saguri Shrine is located in Sayama Saguri and is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; it is considered to be the "Saguri deity" mentioned in the entry for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 (859) in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. From the medieval period to the Meiji era, because it enshrined a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}, it was called Atemoto (Ippin) Hachimangū. It appears under that name in the late-Edo gazetteer {{ill|lt=Miyako Meisho Zue|Q11643495|WD=Q11643495|1=Miyako meisho zue}}, where it is described as "about twenty chō west of Ōkubo, within Sayama village"{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.kumiyama.lg.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=333&frmCd=3-3-1-0-0 |website=Kumiyama Town|title=Saguri Shrine|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Precincts === === Honden === The scale and style of the honden are three-bay nagare-zukuri (a honden with three front bays), reflecting enshrinement of a divided spirit from Iwashimizu Hachimangū. According to shrine tradition, the honden was likely built around 1494 ({{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 3), at the end of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress-bark roofing), and has a {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} at the front. Between the bracket complexes of the honden are carved {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} struts: "flowers and birds" on the left and "autumn leaves and deer" on the right. Above the side panels are intricate carvings featuring "squirrels and grapes." In 1980-1981 (Shōwa 55-56), repainting and reroofing were carried out. === Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === North of the honden stands a giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}, about 30 m tall with a trunk circumference of 535 cm, estimated at 400-500 years old. A {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} is wrapped around it, and a small Inari shrine at its base shows that local residents have long revered it as a sacred tree. Because this lowland area was frequently flooded, large trees are relatively rare; together with its vigorous growth, this makes it highly valuable. In June 1993 (Heisei 3), it was selected for "Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites (plant category)," and in February 1994 (Heisei 8) it was designated a town natural monument{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/flora44.html |title=Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites: camphor tree of Saguri Shrine |website=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Saguri Shrine honden === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (national registration) === * Saguri Shrine haiden, honden gate, tamagaki fence, and stone torii (structures) - nationally registered in April 2014 (Heisei 26) === Kumiyama Town designated natural monument === * Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 ;Access * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} / Keihan Uji Kōtsū Bus, get off at "Kumiyama Danchi-guchi," then about 2 minutes on foot north === Footnotes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saguri Tenjin Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} - A shrine in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ujitawara|Q1203224|WD=Q1203224|1=Ujitawara}}. Like Saguri Shrine, it is a shikinaisha. {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:久御山町の建築物]] [[Category:久御山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:山城国の式内社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

It appears under that name in the late-Edo gazetteer {{ill|lt=Miyako Meisho Zue|Q11643495|WD=Q11643495|1=Miyako meisho zue}}, where it is described as "about twenty chō west of Ōkubo, within Sayama village"{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.kumiyama.lg.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=333&frmCd=3-3-1-0-0 |website=Kumiyama Town|title=Saguri Shrine|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Precincts === === Honden === The scale and style of the honden are three-bay nagare-zukuri (a honden with three front bays), reflecting enshrinement of a divided spirit from Iwashimizu Hachimangū. According to shrine tradition, the honden was likely built around 1494 ({{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 3), at the end of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress-bark roofing), and has a {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} at the front. Between the bracket complexes of the honden are carved {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} struts: "flowers and birds" on the left and "autumn leaves and deer" on the right. Above the side panels are intricate carvings featuring "squirrels and grapes." In 1980-1981 (Shōwa 55-56), repainting and reroofing were carried out. === Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === North of the honden stands a giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}, about 30 m tall with a trunk circumference of 535 cm, estimated at 400-500 years old. A {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} is wrapped around it, and a small Inari shrine at its base shows that local residents have long revered it as a sacred tree. Because this lowland area was frequently flooded, large trees are relatively rare; together with its vigorous growth, this makes it highly valuable. In June 1993 (Heisei 3), it was selected for "Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites (plant category)," and in February 1994 (Heisei 8) it was designated a town natural monument{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/flora44.html |title=Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites: camphor tree of Saguri Shrine |website=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Saguri Shrine honden === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (national registration) === * Saguri Shrine haiden, honden gate, tamagaki fence, and stone torii (structures) - nationally registered in April 2014 (Heisei 26) === Kumiyama Town designated natural monument === * Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 ;Access * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} / Keihan Uji Kōtsū Bus, get off at "Kumiyama Danchi-guchi," then about 2 minutes on foot north === Footnotes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saguri Tenjin Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} - A shrine in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ujitawara|Q1203224|WD=Q1203224|1=Ujitawara}}. Like Saguri Shrine, it is a shikinaisha. {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:久御山町の建築物]] [[Category:久御山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]][[Category:qq]]

link|Q55532282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saguri Shrine |画像 = [[File:雙栗神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Atemoto Ippin Hachimangū (former name) |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Saguri Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Saguri Shrine''' (さぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The seven enshrined deities are as follows. * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) *: In the {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} she appears as "Amaterasu Ōkami," and in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as "Amaterasu Ōmikami." She is a deity in {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|1=Japanese mythology}}, famous for the hiding of the heavenly rock cave ({{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}). * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) *: In the Nihon Shoki, written as "Susanoo no Mikoto" and "Susa-no-o no Mikoto"; in the Kojiki, "Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto." This is also a deity in Japanese mythology. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} (ことしろぬし) *: A deity in Japanese mythology, regarded as a child of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (おうじんてんのう) *: Called "Homuta Tennō" in the Nihon Shoki and "Homudawake-no-Mikoto" in the Kojiki. He is considered an emperor likely to have existed from the late {{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099|1=4th century}} to the early {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095|1=5th century}}. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} (ひめがみ) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (にんとくてんのう) * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) === History === Saguri Shrine is located in Sayama Saguri and is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; it is considered to be the "Saguri deity" mentioned in the entry for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 (859) in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. From the medieval period to the Meiji era, because it enshrined a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}, it was called Atemoto (Ippin) Hachimangū. It appears under that name in the late-Edo gazetteer {{ill|lt=Miyako Meisho Zue|Q11643495|WD=Q11643495|1=Miyako meisho zue}}, where it is described as "about twenty chō west of Ōkubo, within Sayama village"{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.kumiyama.lg.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=333&frmCd=3-3-1-0-0 |website=Kumiyama Town|title=Saguri Shrine|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Precincts === === Honden === The scale and style of the honden are three-bay nagare-zukuri (a honden with three front bays), reflecting enshrinement of a divided spirit from Iwashimizu Hachimangū. According to shrine tradition, the honden was likely built around 1494 ({{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 3), at the end of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress-bark roofing), and has a {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} at the front. Between the bracket complexes of the honden are carved {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} struts: "flowers and birds" on the left and "autumn leaves and deer" on the right. Above the side panels are intricate carvings featuring "squirrels and grapes." In 1980-1981 (Shōwa 55-56), repainting and reroofing were carried out. === Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === North of the honden stands a giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}, about 30 m tall with a trunk circumference of 535 cm, estimated at 400-500 years old. A {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} is wrapped around it, and a small Inari shrine at its base shows that local residents have long revered it as a sacred tree. Because this lowland area was frequently flooded, large trees are relatively rare; together with its vigorous growth, this makes it highly valuable. In June 1993 (Heisei 3), it was selected for "Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites (plant category)," and in February 1994 (Heisei 8) it was designated a town natural monument{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/flora44.html |title=Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites: camphor tree of Saguri Shrine |website=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Saguri Shrine honden === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (national registration) === * Saguri Shrine haiden, honden gate, tamagaki fence, and stone torii (structures) - nationally registered in April 2014 (Heisei 26) === Kumiyama Town designated natural monument === * Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 ;Access * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} / Keihan Uji Kōtsū Bus, get off at "Kumiyama Danchi-guchi," then about 2 minutes on foot north === Footnotes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saguri Tenjin Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} - A shrine in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ujitawara|Q1203224|WD=Q1203224|1=Ujitawara}}. Like Saguri Shrine, it is a shikinaisha. {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:久御山町の建築物]] [[Category:久御山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]]

link|Q55532282}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Saguri Shrine |画像 = [[File:雙栗神社 - panoramio.jpg|270px]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Atemoto Ippin Hachimangū (former name) |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Saguri Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Saguri Shrine''' (さぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The seven enshrined deities are as follows. * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} (あまてらすおおみかみ) *: In the {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}} she appears as "Amaterasu Ōkami," and in the {{ill|lt=Kojiki|Q813031|WD=Q813031|1=Kojiki}} as "Amaterasu Ōmikami." She is a deity in {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|1=Japanese mythology}}, famous for the hiding of the heavenly rock cave ({{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}). * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) *: In the Nihon Shoki, written as "Susanoo no Mikoto" and "Susa-no-o no Mikoto"; in the Kojiki, "Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto." This is also a deity in Japanese mythology. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} (ことしろぬし) *: A deity in Japanese mythology, regarded as a child of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (おうじんてんのう) *: Called "Homuta Tennō" in the Nihon Shoki and "Homudawake-no-Mikoto" in the Kojiki. He is considered an emperor likely to have existed from the late {{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099|1=4th century}} to the early {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095|1=5th century}}. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} (ひめがみ) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (にんとくてんのう) * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) === History === Saguri Shrine is located in Sayama Saguri and is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; it is considered to be the "Saguri deity" mentioned in the entry for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 (859) in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. From the medieval period to the Meiji era, because it enshrined a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}, it was called Atemoto (Ippin) Hachimangū. It appears under that name in the late-Edo gazetteer {{ill|lt=Miyako Meisho Zue|Q11643495|WD=Q11643495|1=Miyako meisho zue}}, where it is described as "about twenty chō west of Ōkubo, within Sayama village"{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.kumiyama.lg.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=333&frmCd=3-3-1-0-0 |website=Kumiyama Town|title=Saguri Shrine|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Precincts === === Honden === The scale and style of the honden are three-bay nagare-zukuri (a honden with three front bays), reflecting enshrinement of a divided spirit from Iwashimizu Hachimangū. According to shrine tradition, the honden was likely built around 1494 ({{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 3), at the end of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress-bark roofing), and has a {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} at the front. Between the bracket complexes of the honden are carved {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} struts: "flowers and birds" on the left and "autumn leaves and deer" on the right. Above the side panels are intricate carvings featuring "squirrels and grapes." In 1980-1981 (Shōwa 55-56), repainting and reroofing were carried out. === Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === North of the honden stands a giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}, about 30 m tall with a trunk circumference of 535 cm, estimated at 400-500 years old. A {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} is wrapped around it, and a small Inari shrine at its base shows that local residents have long revered it as a sacred tree. Because this lowland area was frequently flooded, large trees are relatively rare; together with its vigorous growth, this makes it highly valuable. In June 1993 (Heisei 3), it was selected for "Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites (plant category)," and in February 1994 (Heisei 8) it was designated a town natural monument{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/flora44.html |title=Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites: camphor tree of Saguri Shrine |website=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Saguri Shrine honden === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (national registration) === * Saguri Shrine haiden, honden gate, tamagaki fence, and stone torii (structures) - nationally registered in April 2014 (Heisei 26) === Kumiyama Town designated natural monument === * Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 ;Access * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} / Keihan Uji Kōtsū Bus, get off at "Kumiyama Danchi-guchi," then about 2 minutes on foot north === Footnotes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saguri Tenjin Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} - A shrine in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ujitawara|Q1203224|WD=Q1203224|1=Ujitawara}}. Like Saguri Shrine, it is a shikinaisha. {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:久御山町の建築物]] [[Category:久御山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]]

She is a deity in {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|WD=Q814158|1=Japanese mythology}}, famous for the hiding of the heavenly rock cave ({{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato|Q3231835|WD=Q3231835|1=Amano-Iwato}}). * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (すさのおのみこと) *: In the Nihon Shoki, written as "Susanoo no Mikoto" and "Susa-no-o no Mikoto"; in the Kojiki, "Takehaya Susanoo no Mikoto." This is also a deity in Japanese mythology. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} (ことしろぬし) *: A deity in Japanese mythology, regarded as a child of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (おうじんてんのう) *: Called "Homuta Tennō" in the Nihon Shoki and "Homudawake-no-Mikoto" in the Kojiki. He is considered an emperor likely to have existed from the late {{ill|lt=4th century|Q8099|WD=Q8099|1=4th century}} to the early {{ill|lt=5th century|Q8095|WD=Q8095|1=5th century}}. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} (ひめがみ) * {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (にんとくてんのう) * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (じんぐうこうごう) === History === Saguri Shrine is located in Sayama Saguri and is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}; it is considered to be the "Saguri deity" mentioned in the entry for the 27th day of the 1st month of {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 (859) in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. From the medieval period to the Meiji era, because it enshrined a divided spirit of {{ill|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Q710098|WD=Q710098|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}}, it was called Atemoto (Ippin) Hachimangū. It appears under that name in the late-Edo gazetteer {{ill|lt=Miyako Meisho Zue|Q11643495|WD=Q11643495|1=Miyako meisho zue}}, where it is described as "about twenty chō west of Ōkubo, within Sayama village"{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.kumiyama.lg.jp/contents_detail.php?co=cat&frmId=333&frmCd=3-3-1-0-0 |website=Kumiyama Town|title=Saguri Shrine|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Precincts === === Honden === The scale and style of the honden are three-bay nagare-zukuri (a honden with three front bays), reflecting enshrinement of a divided spirit from Iwashimizu Hachimangū. According to shrine tradition, the honden was likely built around 1494 ({{ill|lt=Meiō|Q1193841|WD=Q1193841|1=Meiō}} 3), at the end of the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. The roof is {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress-bark roofing), and has a {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|WD=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} at the front. Between the bracket complexes of the honden are carved {{ill|lt=kaerumata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} struts: "flowers and birds" on the left and "autumn leaves and deer" on the right. Above the side panels are intricate carvings featuring "squirrels and grapes." In 1980-1981 (Shōwa 55-56), repainting and reroofing were carried out. === Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === North of the honden stands a giant {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}}, about 30 m tall with a trunk circumference of 535 cm, estimated at 400-500 years old. A {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} is wrapped around it, and a small Inari shrine at its base shows that local residents have long revered it as a sacred tree. Because this lowland area was frequently flooded, large trees are relatively rare; together with its vigorous growth, this makes it highly valuable. In June 1993 (Heisei 3), it was selected for "Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites (plant category)," and in February 1994 (Heisei 8) it was designated a town natural monument{{Cite web|url=https://www.pref.kyoto.jp/select200/flora44.html |title=Kyoto's 200 Natural Sites: camphor tree of Saguri Shrine |website=Kyoto Prefecture|accessdate=2018-06-02}}. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national designation) === * Saguri Shrine honden === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (national registration) === * Saguri Shrine haiden, honden gate, tamagaki fence, and stone torii (structures) - nationally registered in April 2014 (Heisei 26) === Kumiyama Town designated natural monument === * Camphor tree of Saguri Shrine === Local information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kuse District|Q1076972|WD=Q1076972|1=Kuse District, Kyoto}}{{ill|lt=Kumiyama|Q1203490|WD=Q1203490|1=Kumiyama, Kyoto}}Sayama Saguri 55 ;Access * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|lt=Keihan Bus|Q11375813|WD=Q11375813|1=Keihan Bus}} / Keihan Uji Kōtsū Bus, get off at "Kumiyama Danchi-guchi," then about 2 minutes on foot north === Footnotes === === Sources === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saguri Tenjin Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} - A shrine in {{ill|lt=Tsuzuki District|Q1154678|WD=Q1154678|1=Tsuzuki District, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ujitawara|Q1203224|WD=Q1203224|1=Ujitawara}}. Like Saguri Shrine, it is a shikinaisha. {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Saguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:久御山町の建築物]] [[Category:久御山町の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamashiro Province]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:京都府の登録有形文化財]]
